purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows focal uptake in the left lower chest outside the rib, concerning for lung metastasis in this patient with primary osteosarcoma of the femur. Note the residual activity in a renal calyx due to normal excretion of radiotracer.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT of the same patient shows multiple bilateral pulmonary metastases .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows uptake of tracer projecting over the left chest wall.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Left anterior oblique view of the same patient shows focal activity to localize to the left scapula. Oblique and lateral views in addition to the anterior and posterior views help in further accurate localization. The bone scan appearance is nonspecific but was proven to be an osteosarcoma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m bone scan in a skeletally immature patient with a left humeral mass shows a long segment lesion in the left humerus with heterogeneous uptake. The scintigraphic findings are nonspecific, but no other lesions are seen.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Corresponding coronal STIR MR shows a large, aggressive lesion in the humerus with involvement of the adjacent soft tissues . Findings are compatible with Ewing sarcoma. MR allows better evaluation of lesion extent.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior and posterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows double density sign with a hot spot surrounded by warm uptake at the mid shaft of the left tibia, suggestive of osteoid osteoma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT of the left lower extremity in the same patient shows the lesion with central nidus surrounded by lucency and sclerosis in the mid shaft of left tibia.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT through the pelvis shows a large, destructive soft tissue mass in the sacrum of this patient with clinical findings of multiple myeloma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Fused F-18 FDG PET/CT at the same level shows intense uptake within this lesion that is compatible with an active myelomatous lesion.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan in a skeletally immature patient shows a focus of increased activity within the right mandible. This was biopsy-proven giant cell tumor (GCT).
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT (bone window) of the same patient shows an expansile lesion with mixed soft tissue heterogeneity and wispy septations within the right parasymphyseal region and body of the mandible.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior and posterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows increased tracer uptake in an enchondroma of the right femur . There are scattered arthritic/degenerative changes without evidence for polyostotic disease.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: AP radiograph of the right distal femur in the same patient shows a ring and arc matrix lesion in the distal femoral diaphysis spanning ~ 9 cm in the craniocaudal dimension. This is consistent with an enchondroma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior and posterior whole-body Tc-99m MDP bone scan in a patient with known GCT shows focal increased uptake in the left lateral femoral condyle .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: AP radiograph of the left knee in the same patient shows a lytic expansile lesion in the lateral femoral condyle compatible with GCT.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior and posterior whole-body Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows a large region of uptake involving the right proximal femur . The uptake appears to extend beyond the expected cortical margins of the proximal femur. No polyostotic disease is seen.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal CT in the same patient shows an exophytic mass with contiguous cortex and medullary cavity with the femoral neck (not shown). Findings are compatible with a solitary osteochondroma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Posterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows multiple foci of activity involving the right iliac crest, posterior left 3rd rib, and left scapula. These findings were proven to be hereditary multiple exostoses.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior and posterior whole-body Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows multiple long segment lesions with predominant left-sided involvement. Deformities of the left upper and lower extremities are also shown . These findings are most compatible with polyostotic fibrous dysplasia.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: F-18 FDG PET/CT for assessment of treatment response in a patient with multiple myeloma shows lytic lesions in sacrum and bilateral ilium without significant F-18 FDG uptake. Findings are compatible with treated multiple myeloma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: F-18 FDG PET/CT in a patient with osteosarcoma shows high tracer uptake in the primary on PET and fused PET/CT . CT shows an expansile lesion with bone and soft tissue involvement. No metastases were found.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m bone scan in a skeletally immature patient with a left humeral mass shows a long segment lesion in the left humerus with heterogeneous uptake. The scintigraphic findings are nonspecific, but no other lesions are seen.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Corresponding coronal STIR MR shows a large, aggressive lesion in the humerus with involvement of the adjacent soft tissues . Findings are compatible with Ewing sarcoma. MR allows better evaluation of lesion extent.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: F-18 FDG PET/CT in a patient with mandibular osteosarcoma shows high tracer uptake in the primary on PET , fused PET/CT , & MIP image . CT shows an expansile lesion with bone and soft tissue involvement. No metastases were found.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Coronal F-18 FDG PET/CT shows lytic lesions with varying treatment response in this patient with multiple myeloma, including in the proximal right tibia with moderate metabolic activity and in the proximal left tibia and distal left femur with mild activity.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior (left) and posterior (right) Tc-99m MDP bone scan shows double-density sign with a hot spot surrounded by warm uptake at the midshaft of the left tibia, suggestive of osteoid osteoma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT of the left lower extremity in the same patient shows the lesion with central nidus surrounded by lucency and sclerosis in the midshaft of the left tibia.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial CT through the pelvis shows a large, destructive soft tissue mass in the sacrum of this patient with clinical findings of multiple myeloma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Fused F-18 FDG PET/CT at the same level shows intense uptake within this lesion, compatible with an active myelomatous lesion. Tc-99m MDP bone scans are less sensitive for lytic lesions.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m MDP bone scan in a patient with chondrosarcoma of the left femur post resection and fixation shows ↑ uptake in the proximal and distal femur. Additional imaging is necessary to differentiate between hardware-induced uptake and skip metastasis.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Anterior Tc-99m MDP scan (left) shows tracer uptake in the left chest . Oblique and lateral views help in further localization. LAO view shows focal activity localizing to the left scapula. This was histologically proven to be an osteosarcoma.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.
